role: Deputy leader of MEK; commander of MEK armed wing
bio: Moussa Khiabani studied physics at the University of Tehran and joined the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq). He trained in guerrilla warfare in Lebanon and was sentenced to life imprisonment in 1972 for MEK activities. Released in January 1979 during the revolution, he became [Massoud Rajavi](/people/massoud-rajavi)'s deputy and commanded the MEK's armed wing inside Iran. On 8 February 1982, the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) raided his safehouse in northern Tehran. After a three-hour firefight, Khiabani was killed along with [Ashraf Rabiei](/people/ashraf-rabiei).
editorial_notes: His wife Azar Rezaei also killed in the same raid. His sister Azar Khiabani later married Mehdi Abrishamchi.
